title: Add trust-flush CronJob sub-reconciler
description:
# GitHub Issue #156: Add trust-flush CronJob sub-reconciler

**Category**: feature | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Add a `reconcileTrustFlush` sub-reconciler that creates a CronJob running `keystone-manage trust_flush` on a configurable schedule. Expose configuration via new CRD fields under `spec.trustFlush` with `schedule` (cron expression, default `0 * * * *`), optional `args` (additional CLI flags such as `--keystone-user`, `--keystone-group`, `--date`), and `suspend` (boolean, default `false`). The CronJob should reuse the existing Keystone image, mount the config ConfigMap and credential/fernet key Secrets read-only, and apply the same SecurityContext as the other Job/CronJob workloads.

**Motivation**: Keystone stores trust delegations in the `trust` table. Expired trusts are never automatically removed, so this table grows unboundedly over the lifetime of a deployment. In long-running environments this wastes database storage, slows queries on the trust table, and complicates audit. A periodic `keystone-manage trust_flush` purges expired trust records and keeps the database clean.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` (new `TrustFlush` struct)
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go` (default values, validation)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go` (call new sub-reconciler)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush.go` (new file)
- `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` (CRD regeneration)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `spec.trustFlush.schedule` accepts a cron expression (default `0 * * * *`)
- [ ] `spec.trustFlush.suspend` allows pausing the CronJob without deleting it
- [ ] `spec.trustFlush.args` passes additional flags to `keystone-manage trust_flush`
- [ ] CronJob uses the same image, config mounts, and SecurityContext as existing CronJobs
- [ ] New `TrustFlushReady` condition is added and reflected in the aggregate Ready condition
- [ ] CronJob is not created when `spec.trustFlush` is omitted (opt-in)
- [ ] Unit tests cover CronJob creation, suspension toggle, and custom args
- [ ] Webhook sets defaults and validates the cron expression

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/156

stories.title: Operator enables periodic trust flush
stories.want: to enable periodic trust flush by adding spec.trustFlush to my Keystone CR
stories.so_that: expired trust delegations are automatically purged from the database, preventing unbounded table growth
criteria: Adding spec.trustFlush to the Keystone CR creates a CronJob named {name}-trust-flush
The CronJob runs keystone-manage trust_flush with --config-dir=/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/
The CronJob uses the same Keystone image as specified in spec.image
TrustFlushReady condition is set to True after the CronJob is configured

stories.title: Operator configures custom trust flush schedule
stories.want: to set a custom cron schedule for trust flush
stories.so_that: purging happens at times appropriate for my environment's load patterns
criteria: spec.trustFlush.schedule accepts any valid 5-field cron expression
Default schedule is '0 * * * *' (hourly) when not explicitly set
Changing the schedule updates the CronJob spec without recreating it
Invalid cron expressions are rejected by the validating webhook with a descriptive error

stories.title: Operator suspends trust flush without removing config
stories.want: to suspend the trust flush CronJob via spec.trustFlush.suspend
stories.so_that: I can temporarily halt purging during maintenance windows without losing my configuration
criteria: Setting spec.trustFlush.suspend to true sets the CronJob's spec.suspend to true
Setting spec.trustFlush.suspend back to false resumes the CronJob
The CronJob resource is not deleted when suspended
TrustFlushReady condition remains True when suspended

stories.title: Operator passes custom flags to trust_flush
stories.want: to pass additional CLI flags to keystone-manage trust_flush via spec.trustFlush.args
stories.so_that: I can target specific users, groups, or date thresholds for selective purging
criteria: spec.trustFlush.args accepts a string slice of additional CLI flags
Args are appended after 'keystone-manage trust_flush' in the container command
Omitting args runs trust_flush with no additional flags (default behavior)
Args such as '--keystone-user', '--keystone-group', '--date' are passed through verbatim

stories.title: Trust flush is opt-in and does not affect existing CRs
stories.want: trust flush to be entirely optional
stories.so_that: existing Keystone CRs without spec.trustFlush continue to work unchanged
criteria: When spec.trustFlush is omitted (nil), no trust-flush CronJob is created
TrustFlushReady condition is set to True with reason 'TrustFlushNotRequired' when omitted
The aggregate Ready condition is not blocked by trust flush when omitted
Removing spec.trustFlush from a CR deletes the previously created CronJob

stories.title: CronJob uses secure container configuration
stories.want: the trust flush CronJob to use the same SecurityContext and volume mounts as existing CronJobs
stories.so_that: the security posture is consistent across all operator-managed workloads
criteria: Container runs with restrictedSecurityContext (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities, seccomp RuntimeDefault)
Config ConfigMap is mounted read-only at /etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/
Fernet keys Secret is mounted read-only at /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/
Credential keys Secret is mounted read-only at /etc/keystone/credential-keys/

stories.title: System reflects trust flush status in conditions
stories.want: a TrustFlushReady condition that participates in the aggregate Ready condition
stories.so_that: operators and monitoring systems can observe the trust flush sub-reconciler state
criteria: TrustFlushReady is listed in subConditionTypes and checked by aggregateReady
TrustFlushReady=True when CronJob is configured or when trust flush is not required
TrustFlushReady=False is never set in normal operation (errors propagate via reconcile error)
The aggregate Ready condition includes TrustFlushReady in its evaluation

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL create a CronJob running keystone-manage trust_flush when spec.trustFlush is set
requirements.rationale: Core feature: periodic purge of expired trust delegations via a Kubernetes CronJob

scenarios.name: CronJob created when trustFlush is set
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created with spec.trustFlush set (e.g. trustFlush: {})
scenarios.then: the reconciler creates a CronJob named {name}-trust-flush in the same namespace

scenarios.name: CronJob updated when trustFlush spec changes
scenarios.when: the spec.trustFlush fields are modified on an existing Keystone CR
scenarios.then: the reconciler updates the existing CronJob spec to match the new desired state
and_then: no new CronJob is created; the existing one is updated in-place via EnsureCronJob

scenarios.name: CronJob uses Keystone service image
scenarios.when: the CronJob is created or updated
scenarios.then: the container image is spec.image.repository:spec.image.tag
and_then: the image matches the Deployment and other CronJob images

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL NOT create a trust flush CronJob when spec.trustFlush is nil (opt-in behavior)
requirements.rationale: Existing CRs without spec.trustFlush must not be affected; the feature is opt-in

scenarios.name: No CronJob when trustFlush is omitted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created without spec.trustFlush
scenarios.then: no trust-flush CronJob is created in the namespace
and_then: TrustFlushReady condition is set to True with reason 'TrustFlushNotRequired'

scenarios.name: CronJob deleted when trustFlush is removed
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush is removed from an existing Keystone CR that previously had it
scenarios.then: the existing trust-flush CronJob is deleted
and_then: TrustFlushReady condition is set to True with reason 'TrustFlushNotRequired'

scenarios.name: Aggregate Ready not blocked when trustFlush omitted
scenarios.when: all other sub-conditions are True and spec.trustFlush is nil
scenarios.then: the aggregate Ready condition is True
and_then: TrustFlushReady=True (not required) does not block the aggregate

requirements.description: The CRD SHALL expose spec.trustFlush.schedule as a cron expression defaulting to '0 * * * *'
requirements.rationale: Operators need to control when trust flush runs; hourly default is a sensible baseline

scenarios.name: Default schedule applied
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created with spec.trustFlush: {} (empty)
scenarios.then: spec.trustFlush.schedule is defaulted to '0 * * * *' by the CRD schema marker

scenarios.name: Custom schedule honored
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created with spec.trustFlush.schedule: '30 2 * * 0'
scenarios.then: the CronJob schedule is '30 2 * * 0'
and_then: the schedule is passed to the CronJob spec verbatim

scenarios.name: Schedule change propagated
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.schedule is changed from '0 * * * *' to '0 */6 * * *'
scenarios.then: the CronJob schedule is updated to '0 */6 * * *'

requirements.description: The CRD SHALL expose spec.trustFlush.suspend to pause the CronJob without deleting it
requirements.rationale: Operators need to temporarily halt purging during maintenance without losing configuration

scenarios.name: Suspend pauses CronJob
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.suspend is set to true
scenarios.then: the CronJob spec.suspend is set to &true (pointer to true)

scenarios.name: Unsuspend resumes CronJob
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.suspend is changed from true to false
scenarios.then: the CronJob spec.suspend is set to &false (pointer to false)
and_then: scheduled runs resume according to the schedule

scenarios.name: Default is not suspended
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush is set without specifying suspend
scenarios.then: the CronJob is not suspended (suspend defaults to false / &false)

requirements.description: The CRD SHALL expose spec.trustFlush.args to pass additional CLI flags to keystone-manage trust_flush
requirements.rationale: Operators may need to target specific users, groups, or dates for selective purging

scenarios.name: Args appended to command
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.args is set to ['--keystone-user', 'keystone', '--keystone-group', 'keystone']
scenarios.then: the container command includes these flags after trust_flush

scenarios.name: No args by default
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.args is omitted or empty
scenarios.then: the container command ends with 'trust_flush' and no additional flags

scenarios.name: Date flag passed through
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.args is set to ['--date', '2024-01-01']
scenarios.then: the container command includes '--date 2024-01-01' after trust_flush

requirements.description: The trust flush CronJob SHALL use the same image, SecurityContext, config mounts, and labels as existing CronJobs
requirements.rationale: Consistent security posture and operational behavior across all operator-managed workloads

scenarios.name: SecurityContext matches restricted profile
scenarios.when: the trust flush CronJob is created
scenarios.then: the container SecurityContext uses restrictedSecurityContext() (runAsNonRoot=true, runAsUser/Group=42424, readOnlyRootFilesystem=true, drop ALL, seccomp RuntimeDefault)

scenarios.name: Volumes mounted read-only
scenarios.when: the trust flush CronJob is created
scenarios.then: config ConfigMap, fernet-keys Secret, and credential-keys Secret are mounted read-only

scenarios.name: Labels follow commonLabels pattern
scenarios.when: the trust flush CronJob is created
scenarios.then: the CronJob and pod template have labels app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone, app.kubernetes.io/instance={name}, app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=keystone-operator

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL add TrustFlushReady to the aggregate Ready condition
requirements.rationale: The aggregate Ready condition must reflect all sub-reconciler states for accurate status reporting

scenarios.name: TrustFlushReady included in subConditionTypes
scenarios.when: the aggregateReady function evaluates conditions
scenarios.then: TrustFlushReady is included in the condition types checked by conditions.AllTrue()

scenarios.name: Aggregate Ready requires TrustFlushReady
scenarios.when: all conditions except TrustFlushReady are True
scenarios.then: the aggregate Ready condition is False
and_then: Ready=True only when TrustFlushReady is also True

scenarios.name: reconcileTrustFlush called in Reconcile chain
scenarios.when: the main Reconcile function executes
scenarios.then: reconcileTrustFlush is called after reconcileBootstrap and before setReadyCondition
and_then: errors from reconcileTrustFlush are propagated via updateStatus

requirements.description: The validating webhook SHALL reject invalid cron expressions in spec.trustFlush.schedule
requirements.rationale: Defense-in-depth validation prevents misconfigured CronJobs that would never fire

scenarios.name: Valid cron expression accepted
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.schedule is set to a valid 5-field cron expression (e.g. '0 */6 * * *')
scenarios.then: the validating webhook accepts the Keystone CR

scenarios.name: Invalid cron expression rejected
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.schedule is set to an invalid expression (e.g. 'not-a-cron')
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns a 422 error with field path spec.trustFlush.schedule and the parse error

scenarios.name: Empty schedule rejected
scenarios.when: spec.trustFlush.schedule is empty string (bypassing CRD schema default)
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns a Required error for spec.trustFlush.schedule
and_then: message indicates the default value for reference

requirements.description: The CronJob SHALL have RestartPolicy OnFailure and owner reference to the Keystone CR
requirements.rationale: Consistent with existing CronJobs; owner reference enables garbage collection on CR deletion

scenarios.name: RestartPolicy is OnFailure
scenarios.when: the trust flush CronJob is created
scenarios.then: the pod template spec has restartPolicy: OnFailure

scenarios.name: Owner reference set to Keystone CR
scenarios.when: the trust flush CronJob is created via EnsureCronJob
scenarios.then: the CronJob has an ownerReference pointing to the Keystone CR
and_then: the CronJob is garbage-collected when the Keystone CR is deleted

scenarios.name: CronJob labels follow commonLabels pattern
scenarios.when: the trust flush CronJob is created
scenarios.then: the CronJob and pod template have standard app.kubernetes.io labels
and_then: labels match the Deployment and other CronJobs for consistent querying

tasks.title: Add TrustFlushSpec struct and optional pointer field to KeystoneSpec (REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005)
tasks.description: Add a new TrustFlushSpec struct to operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go with three fields: Schedule (string, +kubebuilder:default="0 * * * *"), Args ([]string, optional), and Suspend (bool, +kubebuilder:default=false). Add an optional pointer field `TrustFlush *TrustFlushSpec` to KeystoneSpec with +optional marker. Run `make generate` from operators/keystone/ to regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go. Follow the pattern of FernetSpec (lines 200-210) for field annotations and UWSGISpec (lines 180-198) for optional pointer semantics.

tasks.title: Add webhook validation for spec.trustFlush cron expression with unit tests (REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go validate(), add a block guarded by `if k.Spec.TrustFlush != nil` that validates the Schedule field: (1) reject empty schedule with field.Required, (2) parse with cron.ParseStandard and reject invalid expressions with field.Invalid. Follow the exact pattern used for Fernet RotationSchedule validation (lines 189-200). No webhook Default() changes needed — schedule default is handled by the CRD schema marker (same pattern as Fernet RotationSchedule, per plan decision in CC-0011). Add unit tests in keystone_webhook_test.go: (a) valid cron accepted, (b) invalid cron rejected, (c) empty schedule rejected when trustFlush is non-nil, (d) trustFlush nil passes validation, (e) validKeystone() helper extended with TrustFlush nil (no regression). Follow test naming conventions from existing webhook tests.

tasks.title: Create reconcile_trustflush.go with reconcileTrustFlush and trustFlushCronJob builder (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush.go with: (1) reconcileTrustFlush(ctx, keystone, configMapName) (ctrl.Result, error) — follows the HPA/NetworkPolicy opt-in pattern: when spec.TrustFlush is nil, delete any existing CronJob via deleteTrustFlushCronJob (private helper using client.IgnoreNotFound pattern from deleteNetworkPolicy at reconcile_networkpolicy.go:291-298) and set TrustFlushReady=True/TrustFlushNotRequired; when set, build CronJob via trustFlushCronJob() and call job.EnsureCronJob(), then set TrustFlushReady=True/TrustFlushConfigured. (2) trustFlushCronJob(keystone, configMapName) *batchv1.CronJob — builds CronJob with: name={name}-trust-flush, schedule from spec, suspend from spec (as *bool pointer), image from spec, single container 'trust-flush' with command built as []string{'keystone-manage', '--config-dir=/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/', 'trust_flush'} + args, SecurityContext from restrictedSecurityContext(), RestartPolicy OnFailure, volumes: config (ConfigMap ro), fernet-keys (Secret ro), credential-keys (Secret ro). Follow fernetRotationCronJob (reconcile_fernet.go:226-324) for structure but simpler — no init containers, no RBAC, no env vars, no PushSecret.

tasks.title: Wire reconcileTrustFlush into keystone_controller.go (REQ-007)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go: (1) Add 'TrustFlushReady' to the subConditionTypes slice (after 'BootstrapReady'). (2) Add the reconcileTrustFlush call in the Reconcile() function after the reconcileBootstrap block and before setReadyCondition, following the exact same pattern: `if result, err := r.reconcileTrustFlush(ctx, &keystone, configMapName); !result.IsZero() || err != nil { return r.updateStatus(ctx, &keystone, result, err) }`. No RBAC marker changes needed (batch/cronjobs already in the existing markers at line 58).

tasks.title: Unit tests for reconcileTrustFlush: creation, opt-in, suspend, args, spec, conditions (REQ-001 through REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush_test.go with tests following the fernet/HPA test patterns. Include: trustFlushTestScheme() and trustFlushTestKeystone() helpers (like fernetTestScheme/fernetTestKeystone). Tests: (1) TestReconcileTrustFlush_Nil_SetsTrustFlushNotRequired — spec.TrustFlush nil, verify no CronJob created, condition TrustFlushReady=True/TrustFlushNotRequired [unit]. (2) TestReconcileTrustFlush_Set_CreatesCronJob — spec.TrustFlush set with default schedule, verify CronJob created with correct name/schedule [unit]. (3) TestReconcileTrustFlush_SuspendToggle — verify CronJob suspend field reflects spec [unit]. (4) TestReconcileTrustFlush_CustomArgs — verify args appended to container command [unit]. (5) TestReconcileTrustFlush_ScheduleUpdated — pre-create CronJob with old schedule, verify updated [unit]. (6) TestReconcileTrustFlush_CronJobSpec — verify full CronJob spec: image, SecurityContext (via expectRestrictedSecurityContext helper), volumes, mounts, labels, RestartPolicy [unit]. (7) TestReconcileTrustFlush_ConditionMessages — verify condition type/status/reason/message/observedGeneration [unit]. (8) TestReconcileTrustFlush_Nil_DeletesExistingCronJob — pre-create CronJob, set trustFlush to nil, verify deletion [unit]. (9) TestReconcileTrustFlush_EnsureError_Propagated — use interceptor.Funcs to simulate CronJob creation error, verify error propagation [unit]. (10) TestTrustFlushCronJob_NoArgs — verify command when args is empty [unit]. (11) TestTrustFlushCronJob_WithArgs — verify command when args has values [unit].

tasks.title: Integration tests for trust flush CronJob lifecycle (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Add integration tests to operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go (behind //go:build integration tag). Tests: (1) TestIntegration_TrustFlush_CronJobCreated — create Keystone CR with spec.trustFlush set, verify CronJob is eventually created with correct spec [integration]. (2) TestIntegration_TrustFlush_CronJobDeleted — create Keystone CR with trustFlush, verify CronJob exists, then remove trustFlush from spec, verify CronJob is eventually deleted [integration]. (3) TestIntegration_TrustFlush_OmittedDoesNotCreateCronJob — create Keystone CR without trustFlush, verify no trust-flush CronJob exists [integration]. Follow the existing integration test patterns: use setupEnvTestWithController, integrationBrownfieldKeystone helper (extended with TrustFlush field), Eventually/Consistently assertions with eventuallyTimeout/pollInterval constants. Update integrationBrownfieldKeystone to optionally support TrustFlush or create a dedicated helper.

tasks.title: Run make generate and make manifests to regenerate CRD and deepcopy (REQ-003)
tasks.description: From the operators/keystone/ directory, run `make generate` to regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go with the new TrustFlushSpec DeepCopy methods, and `make manifests` to regenerate the CRD YAML at operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml with the new spec.trustFlush fields. Verify the generated CRD includes the trustFlush property with schedule default, args array, and suspend boolean.

tasks.title: Update Keystone CRD and reconciler reference documentation
tasks.description: Update docs/reference/keystone-crd.md: add spec.trustFlush section to the Resource Shape YAML example with schedule, args, and suspend fields. Add a field table documenting TrustFlushSpec fields with types, defaults, and constraints. Update docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md: add reconcileTrustFlush to the reconciliation flow diagram (after reconcileBootstrap, before Ready), add a subsection describing its behavior (three lifecycle paths: set, nil, error). Update architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md: add reconcileTrustFlush to the ASCII reconciliation flow diagram and add a brief subsection.

test_specifications.story: Trust flush is opt-in and does not affect existing CRs
test_specifications.expected: When spec.TrustFlush is nil, no CronJob is created and TrustFlushReady condition is True with reason TrustFlushNotRequired

test_specifications.story: Trust flush is opt-in and does not affect existing CRs
test_specifications.expected: When spec.TrustFlush is nil but a trust-flush CronJob exists, the CronJob is deleted

test_specifications.story: Operator enables periodic trust flush

test_specifications.story: Operator suspends trust flush without removing config
test_specifications.expected: CronJob spec.suspend reflects spec.trustFlush.suspend value (true/false)

test_specifications.story: Operator passes custom flags to trust_flush
test_specifications.expected: Container command includes trust_flush followed by the specified args

test_specifications.story: Operator configures custom trust flush schedule
test_specifications.expected: Changing the schedule in the spec updates the CronJob schedule in-place

test_specifications.story: CronJob uses secure container configuration
test_specifications.expected: Full CronJob spec matches: correct image, restrictedSecurityContext, read-only volume mounts, commonLabels, RestartPolicy OnFailure

test_specifications.story: System reflects trust flush status in conditions
test_specifications.expected: TrustFlushReady condition has correct type, status, reason, message, and observedGeneration

test_specifications.story: System reflects trust flush status in conditions
test_specifications.expected: Errors from EnsureCronJob are wrapped and propagated to the caller

test_specifications.story: Operator passes custom flags to trust_flush
test_specifications.expected: When args is empty, command is ['keystone-manage', '--config-dir=/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/', 'trust_flush']

test_specifications.story: Operator passes custom flags to trust_flush
test_specifications.expected: When args is ['--date', '2024-01-01'], command includes those flags after trust_flush

test_specifications.story: CronJob uses secure container configuration
test_specifications.expected: Container SecurityContext matches restrictedSecurityContext (via expectRestrictedSecurityContext helper)

test_specifications.story: Operator configures custom trust flush schedule
test_specifications.expected: A valid cron expression in spec.trustFlush.schedule passes validation

test_specifications.story: Operator configures custom trust flush schedule
test_specifications.expected: An invalid cron expression is rejected with a descriptive error on spec.trustFlush.schedule

test_specifications.story: Operator configures custom trust flush schedule
test_specifications.expected: An empty schedule string is rejected with a Required field error

test_specifications.story: Trust flush is opt-in and does not affect existing CRs
test_specifications.expected: When spec.trustFlush is nil, no validation errors are produced for trustFlush fields

test_specifications.story: Operator configures custom trust flush schedule
test_specifications.expected: When spec.TrustFlush is set with schedule '0 * * * *' (default), the CronJob schedule matches

test_specifications.story: Operator configures custom trust flush schedule
test_specifications.expected: When spec.TrustFlush.Schedule is '30 2 * * 0', the CronJob schedule is '30 2 * * 0'

test_specifications.story: Operator suspends trust flush without removing config
test_specifications.expected: When spec.TrustFlush.Suspend is true, the CronJob spec.suspend is pointer-to-true

test_specifications.story: Operator suspends trust flush without removing config
test_specifications.expected: When spec.TrustFlush.Suspend is false (default), the CronJob spec.suspend is pointer-to-false

test_specifications.story: System reflects trust flush status in conditions
test_specifications.expected: TrustFlushReady condition's ObservedGeneration matches keystone.Generation for both set and nil paths

test_specifications.story: Operator enables periodic trust flush
test_specifications.expected: CronJob has ownerReference pointing to the Keystone CR (set by EnsureCronJob)

test_specifications.story: Operator enables periodic trust flush
test_specifications.expected: Creating a Keystone CR with spec.trustFlush results in a CronJob eventually appearing with correct spec

test_specifications.story: Trust flush is opt-in and does not affect existing CRs

test_specifications.story: Trust flush is opt-in and does not affect existing CRs
test_specifications.expected: Creating a Keystone CR without spec.trustFlush does not create a trust-flush CronJob

review_criteria: All 9 SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-009) have corresponding passing unit tests
TrustFlushSpec struct has correct kubebuilder markers (+default, +optional) and matches existing field annotation patterns
Webhook validation follows the fernet RotationSchedule pattern: no Default() changes, validate() guards with nil check, uses cron.ParseStandard
reconcileTrustFlush follows the three-path optional lifecycle (nil → delete + NotRequired, set → ensure + Configured, error → propagate) matching HPA/NetworkPolicy patterns
CronJob spec uses restrictedSecurityContext(), commonLabels(), correct read-only volume mounts matching the Deployment pattern
TrustFlushReady is in subConditionTypes and reconcileTrustFlush is called after reconcileBootstrap in the Reconcile chain
Integration tests cover CronJob creation, deletion on field removal, and opt-in behavior
go vet and golangci-lint pass with no new errors
zz_generated.deepcopy.go and CRD YAML are regenerated and match the Go types
implementation_notes:
Architecture: The trust flush sub-reconciler follows the established three-path optional lifecycle pattern (HPA, NetworkPolicy): nil → delete + condition NotRequired, set → ensure + condition Ready, error → propagate. The CronJob builder follows the fernetRotationCronJob pattern but is simpler — single container, no init containers, no RBAC.

Data flow: Keystone CR spec.trustFlush → reconcileTrustFlush → trustFlushCronJob builder → job.EnsureCronJob → Kubernetes CronJob → scheduled Pod → keystone-manage trust_flush → database purge.

Component diagram:
```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
│ Keystone CR  │────▶│ reconcileTrustFlush   │────▶│ CronJob          │
│ spec.trust   │     │ (nil check, build,    │     │ {name}-trust-    │
│ Flush        │     │  ensure/delete)       │     │ flush            │
└──────────────┘     └──────────────────────┘     └────────┬─────────┘
                                                           │ scheduled
                                                           ▼
                                                  ┌──────────────────┐
                                                  │ Pod              │
                                                  │ keystone-manage  │
                                                  │ trust_flush      │
                                                  │ (+ args)         │
                                                  └──────────────────┘
```

Volumes (all read-only, same as Deployment):
- config: ConfigMap ({configMapName}) at /etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/
- fernet-keys: Secret ({name}-fernet-keys) at /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/
- credential-keys: Secret ({name}-credential-keys) at /etc/keystone/credential-keys/

Potential pitfalls:
1. Suspend field: Kubernetes CronJob spec.suspend is *bool, not bool. Must convert from CRD bool to *bool pointer when building the CronJob spec.
2. Command building: Args are appended to the base command slice, not joined as a string. Use []string for the Command field, not sh -c.
3. Condition ordering: TrustFlushReady must be added to subConditionTypes before the first reconcile after upgrade, or aggregateReady will return false until the condition is set. The reconciler handles this by always setting the condition on every pass.
4. CRD regeneration: make generate must run after types.go changes, make manifests must run after to update CRD YAML.
